name: Coffea (Columnar HEP Analysis Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Coffea Team / Fermilab / CMS

performance.notes: Columnar via awkward-array; Dask or local executors; scales to PB on HTCondor/Dask

agent_readiness.notes: High Energy Physics (HEP) columnar analysis framework. from coffea import processor. from coffea.nanoevents import NanoEventsFactory, NanoAODSchema. events = NanoEventsFactory.from_root('file.root', schemaclass=NanoAODSchema).events(). events.Jet.pt. events.Muon[events.Muon.pt > 20]. class MyProcessor(processor.ProcessorABC): def process(self, events): jets = events.Jet[ak.num(events.Jet) > 0]; return {'n_jets': ak.sum(ak.num(jets))}. runner = processor.Runner(executor=processor.DaskExecutor()). Integrates uproot, awkward-array, hist, vector. NanoAOD, TreeMaker schemas.

gotchas: Domain-specific to High Energy Physics — steep learning curve without HEP background
NanoAOD schema knowledge required for CMS analysis
Coffea 2024 (futures-based) vs Coffea 0.7.x have different APIs
Large ROOT file access requires uproot and XRootD for remote files
Parallel execution with Dask requires cluster setup
best_when: CMS/ATLAS NanoAOD analysis with columnar processing
Scaling HEP event loop analysis to large datasets on HTCondor/Dask
When awkward-array columnar operations replace traditional TTree loops
Reproducible HEP analyses with standardized processor pattern
avoid_when: Simple ROOT file inspection (use uproot directly)
When CMSSW framework is required for full CMS reconstruction
summary: Coffea is a columnar High Energy Physics analysis framework. NanoEventsFactory.from_root().events() → awkward arrays. ProcessorABC pattern. events.Jet.pt, events.Muon[mask]. Dask/local executors for scaling. Integrates uproot, awkward-array, hist. BSD-3 by Fermilab/CMS Team. Domain-specific to HEP/CMS NanoAOD analysis. Steep HEP learning curve. API changed in 2024 version. Scales to PB on HTCondor.
